title: Red Hat Security Advisory: git-lfs security update
overview:
An update for git-lfs is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.2 Update Services for SAP Solutions.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux AppStream E4S (v.9.2)
full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: git-lfs security update

An update for git-lfs is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.2 Update Services for SAP Solutions.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for git-lfs is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.2 Update Services for SAP Solutions.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
git-lfs-3.2.0-2.el9_2.4.src.rpm
git-lfs-3.2.0-2.el9_2.4.x86_64.rpm
git-lfs-debuginfo-3.2.0-2.el9_2.4.x86_64.rpm
git-lfs-debugsource-3.2.0-2.el9_2.4.x86_64.rpm
git-lfs-3.2.0-2.el9_2.4.ppc64le.rpm
git-lfs-debuginfo-3.2.0-2.el9_2.4.ppc64le.rpm
git-lfs-debugsource-3.2.0-2.el9_2.4.ppc64le.rpm
git-lfs-3.2.0-2.el9_2.4.aarch64.rpm
git-lfs-debuginfo-3.2.0-2.el9_2.4.aarch64.rpm
git-lfs-debugsource-3.2.0-2.el9_2.4.aarch64.rpm
git-lfs-3.2.0-2.el9_2.4.s390x.rpm
git-lfs-debuginfo-3.2.0-2.el9_2.4.s390x.rpm
git-lfs-debugsource-3.2.0-2.el9_2.4.s390x.rpm

CVE-2025-26625: A flaw was found in Git LFS. Running git lfs checkout and git lfs pull in a specially crafted repository, specifically with symbolic or hard links tracked by Git LFS and pointing to files outside the working tree or in a bare repository, can cause Git LFS to write to arbitrary file system locations accessible to the user running these commands.
